message.content:
Adversarially review implemented task T124 ROUND 2 (goal G8, defect D42). Return the structured verdict JSON {taskId, verdict:"approve"|"disapprove", criticism:[], questions:[], defects:[], rationale, summary} and WRITE NOTHING to the ledger.

Fetch acceptance: `mcp__ledger__fetch_item` ledger=tasks item=T124.
Branch implement/T124-r2 at commit 0ef749f6e1dbc31754a3dda9aadfe926c087df88, based at main 74f38b7 (verify merge-base==74f38b7). Worktree: /home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/.claude/worktrees/agent-a143fac7b352b7e91.
Diff vs base: `git -C /home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia diff 74f38b7 0ef749f6e1dbc31754a3dda9aadfe926c087df88`.

CONTEXT: YOU raised the round-1 criticism: removeDurableLocked's guard checked only `defIdx >= len(p.probers)` (index-out-of-range), missing the in-range divergence / wrong-entry-splice class the task specified ('any p.probers length diverges from m.defs'). The round-2 fix strengthens the guard to `len(p.probers) != len(m.defs)` and adds an in-range-divergence test.

Verify YOUR criticism is now RESOLVED: (1) the guard covers ALL the cases you named — tail-short in-range (len 2 vs defs 3 + low defIdx), probers LONGER than m.defs, and non-tail desync — read the guard and confirm `len(p.probers) != len(m.defs)` fires before the splice; (2) the new test genuinely reproduces the in-range wrong-entry case and fails under the old guard (re-verify by reverting the guard in a scratch copy if useful); (3) the 3 round-1 tests still pass and the approved core is unchanged; (4) nothing new wrong. Run `cd /home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/.claude/worktrees/agent-a143fac7b352b7e91 && nix develop -c sh -c 'go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./internal/bind/...' && nix develop -c sh -c 'go test -race ./internal/bind/...' && nix develop -c just lint`. If resolved and nothing new wrong, verdict approve. Split criticism[] vs questions[]; file genuine defects.

attachment.content:
- baboon: Baboon data model compiler reference. Covers model syntax, multi-version evolution, code generation, wire-format conversions, database migrations, backward compatibility, and version negotiation. TRIGGER when: designing, changing, or discussing any external API, working with .baboon model files, updating data models, adding new fields or types, bumping model versions, running code generation, implementing conversions, writing database migrations, or discussing API versioning strategy.
- constructive-test-taxonomy: Multi-axis classification of tests (Intention × Encapsulation × Isolation) for evaluating test quality and steering test-writing decisions. Replaces fuzzy Unit/Integration/E2E vocabulary with precise tags and a maintenance-cost heuristic. TRIGGER when: writing new tests, reviewing a test suite, deciding what to test or at what level, discussing mocks vs. real dependencies, classifying tests, or arguing about test coverage or test-strategy tradeoffs.
- dual-tests: Pattern for testing code that touches external systems: write one abstract test suite against an interface, then run it against both the production adapter (real DB/API/FS) and a hand-written in-memory dummy. Prefers manual dummies over auto-generated mocks. TRIGGER when: designing code that depends on a database, HTTP API, message queue, filesystem, or other external system; choosing between mocks, fakes, or dummies; adding integration points; setting up test infrastructure; or when the `constructive-test-taxonomy` skill calls for moving Communication-tier tests toward Blackbox-Atomic.
- environment: Bubblewrap "yolo" sandbox escape workflow (exchange scripts for reaching $HOME, system paths, or files outside the sandbox) plus in-sandbox nix/direnv usage. TRIGGER only when $SMIND_SANDBOXED=1 — i.e. running inside the yolo sandbox and you need to reach something outside it. SKIP entirely when $SMIND_SANDBOXED is unset: you are unsandboxed, so write directly and ignore this skill.
- izumi: Best-practice playbook for the izumi Scala ecosystem — distage (DI), distage-framework (roles/RoleAppMain), distage-testkit (test harness with memoization), distage-framework-docker (test containers), logstage (structured logging), and BIO (bifunctor effect typeclasses). Covers idiomatic bindings, Activation/Axis switching, Lifecycle discipline, plugin layout, and how to apply the `dual-tests` pattern inside distage so one test body runs against both a dummy and a real-DB adapter. TRIGGER when: writing or reviewing Scala code that imports `izumi.distage.*`, `izumi.logstage.*`, `izumi.functional.bio.*`, `distage.*`, `logstage.*`; designing a new distage plugin or Role; wiring a testkit `SpecZIO`/`Spec2`/`Spec1`; configuring `ContainerDef`/`DockerSupportModule`; choosing between `StandardAxis.Repo` or `Scene` tags; or implementing the dual-tests pattern in a distage project. The canonical reference project is https://github.com/7mind/distage-example.
- resilient-ws-ui: Guidelines for building applications that maintain reliable WebSocket connections to a backend and surface connection health clearly to the user. Invoke ONLY when the user explicitly references this skill by name (e.g. "/resilient-ws-ui", "use the resilient-ws-ui skill"). Do NOT auto-invoke based on topic match.
- tass: Target/Actual State Separation (TASS) pattern for reliable state management in event-driven control systems. TRIGGER when: implementing device or entity state management, designing control systems, working with target vs actual state separation, or building event-driven systems with commanded/observed state.
- cq:advance: Advance the WHOLE flow one cycle — chain /cq:investigate:advance, /cq:plan:advance, /cq:implement:advance to quiescence, then report DRAINED / BLOCKED-ON-QUESTIONS / MIXED.
- cq:implement-review: Implement-flow adversarial per-task review rubric + stdout-JSON contract, as a portable promptTemplate for non-Claude harnesses (pi/Codex). Symmetric to cq:plan-review. Judges ONE task's implementation, splits findings into autonomously-fixable criticism[], user-only questions[], and out-of-scope defects[], and emits the verdict as JSON on stdout. Writes NOTHING to the ledger — the orchestrator records the terminal review.
- cq:implement:advance: Advance an implement-flow run one full pass — pick DAG-ready tasks, implement each in an isolated worktree, adversarially review, autonomously fix criticism, register user questions, and merge back in dependency order.
- cq:implement:start: Start an implement-flow run — resolve the target milestones (default: all open ones), validate their task DAG, then hand off to the /cq:implement:advance loop.
- cq:investigate: Start an investigate-flow run — defect intake + bootstrap, then run the /cq:investigate:advance pass inline.
- cq:investigate:advance: Advance an investigate-flow run one research round — read defect state, form/extend the hypothesis tree, dispatch read-only explorers, validate every citation against source, adjudicate node status, and on a confirmed root cause file-and-defer the fix to plan-flow.
- cq:plan: Start a plan-flow goal — create the goal (from free text OR one-or-more idea-ids, one goal per idea), then hand off to the planner for the first clarifying questions.
- cq:plan-review: Shared adversarial plan-review rubric — judge an emitted plan for a goal against the fine-grained / sequenced / testable / grounded / complete rubric, classify findings into new_questions / criticism / defects, and emit a single fenced-json verdict on stdout. The CANONICAL rubric source; Claude's plan-reviewer agent and every non-Claude (Codex/Pi) reviewer judge the same way and emit the same JSON.
- cq:plan:advance: Advance plan-flow goals one full round — a given goal, or (no argument) every unlocked goal — running the planner↔reviewer loop until each needs the user or reaches `planned`.
- cq:plan:follow-up: Add new scope to an EXISTING plan-flow goal — append the follow-up request, re-open the goal, and hand to the planner for a fresh clarifying round.
- cq:planners: Session-only planner-set override — parse a natural-language planner instruction (e.g. "use grok and opus only") into canonical harness:model tokens via the cq.toml [aliases] table (from the ledger MCP get_planners/get_config) plus a documented fallback map; echo the resolved active set; confirm the override is SESSION-ONLY and reverts on the next fresh run.
- cq:reviewers: Session-only reviewer-set override — parse a natural-language reviewer instruction (e.g. "use grok and opus only") into canonical harness:model tokens via the cq.toml [aliases] table (from the ledger MCP get_reviewers/get_config) plus a documented fallback map; echo the resolved active set; confirm the override is SESSION-ONLY and reverts on the next fresh run.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: {"item":{"id":"T124","milestoneId":"M42","status":"planned","fields":{"headline":"Complete the deferred-path multi-peer lifecycle (promoteDeferredLocked fan-out + alignment guard) (D42)","description":"D42's originally-filed AddPath desync is ALREADY partially mitigated (internal/bind/multipath.go ~:2339-2355 mints per-peer probers, keeping every p.probers m.defs-aligned; its comment names D42's panic as 'the pre-fix behaviour'). The LIVE residual is the deferred-path PROMOTION: promoteDeferredLocked (internal/bind/reconcile.go ~:138-188) attaches ONLY the primary's view ('single-peer today' comments), so on a concentrator a promoted deferred path leaves every non-primary peer view-less (its frames on that socket never demux to it) and absent from its scheduler until the next Close→Open. Fix: fan the promotion out to EVERY bound peer, reusing each peer's m.defs-aligned prober (locate by def name/index in p.probers) rather than minting fresh ones, with full rollback on partial failure (mirror attachSharedPathLocked); publish each view via addViewLocked. Add a fail-fast alignment assertion in removeDurableLocked (multipath.go ~:2575) that returns/logs a wiring-defect error instead of silently mis-splicing when any p.probers length diverges from m.defs. Serialized after T123 (shares multipath.go).","acceptance":"`go test -race ./internal/bind/...` passes incl. three new multi-peer deferred-lifecycle regression tests: (a) RemovePath of a deferred path with >=2 bound peers splices every peer's probers correctly (no slice-bounds panic — the D42 scenario), (b) reconcile promotion gives EVERY peer a view + scheduler entry and both peers' DATA flows on the promoted path, (c) Close→Open after a deferred AddPath rebuilds without the out-of-range panic. A test constructing 2 peers + 1 deferred path + RemovePath panics on the pre-fix behaviour and passes after.","suggestedModel":"standard","dependsOn":["T123"],"ledgerRefs":["goals:G8","defects:D42"]},"createdAt":"2026-07-14T09:46:45.633Z","updatedAt":"2026-07-14T09:46:45.633Z","author":"fable-5","session":"671d5adc-7e2a-440e-b87d-6da40edeb7b7"}}

Fix: fan the promotion out to EVERY bound peer, reusing each peer's m.defs-aligned prober (locate by def name/index in p.probers) rather than minting fresh ones, with full rollback on partial failure (mirror attachSharedPathLocked); publish each view via addViewLocked. input.description: Show non-test diff for T124 branch

content.content:
1	package bind
2	
3	import (
4		"net"
5		"net/netip"
6		"syscall"
7		"testing"
8	
9		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/config"
10		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/frame"
11	)
12	
13	// TestRemoveDeferredPathMisalignedPeerProbersFailsFast is the D42 residual regression for
14	// removeDurableLocked's fail-fast alignment guard: on a 2-peer concentrator, if ANY bound
15	// peer's prober set ever falls out of index-alignment with the durable membership (m.defs) —
16	// the class of wiring defect a partial fan-out (a promotion or admission reaching only SOME
17	// peers) produces — removing a path at the misaligned index must return a wiring-defect
18	// error, NOT panic with an index-out-of-range slice operation. It directly simulates the
19	// misalignment (white-box: same package) rather than relying on a currently-reachable code
20	// path, since the OTHER two fixes in this task (promoteDeferredLocked's fan-out, AddPath's
21	// already-shipped fan-out) keep every legitimate admission path aligned — this guards the
22	// invariant defensively, exactly as Open's `pp.prober = p.probers[i]` guard already does for
23	// the read side. Before the removeDurableLocked guard this test PANICS (slice bounds out of
24	// range slicing p.probers[defIdx+1:] past a peer's short probers slice); after it, RemovePath
25	// returns a descriptive error instead.
26	func TestRemoveDeferredPathMisalignedPeerProbersFailsFast(t *testing.T) {
27		pskA := testKey(t, 0x91)
28		pskB := testKey(t, 0x92)
29		clk := newFakeClock()
30		paths := loopbackPaths(1) // boot path "a"
31		m, _, _ := newProbingMultipath(t, paths, pskA, clk)
32	
33		betaSched, betaProbers, betaFactory := concPeerWiring(t, paths, pskB, 0x0BAD1, clk)
34		if err := m.AddConcentratorPeer("beta", pskB, betaSched, betaProbers, betaFactory); err != nil {
35			t.Fatalf("AddConcentratorPeer: %v", err)
36		}
37		if _, _, err := m.Open(0); err != nil {
38			t.Fatalf("Open: %v", err)
39		}
40		t.Cleanup(func() { _ = m.Close() })
41	
42		// A runtime AddPath that DEFERS (EADDRNOTAVAIL): the already-shipped AddPath fan-out mints
43		// a Down prober for EVERY peer, keeping m.defs and every peer's probers aligned at length 2.
44		m.addPathListen = func(_ netip.Addr, _ uint16, _ string) (*net.UDPConn, error) {
45			return nil, syscall.EADDRNOTAVAIL
46		}
47		if err := m.AddPath(config.Path{Name: "deferred-c", SourceAddr: netip.MustParseAddr("127.0.0.1")}); err != nil {
48			t.Fatalf("AddPath (deferred): %v", err)
49		}
50	
51		beta := m.peersByName["beta"]
52		if len(m.defs) != 2 || len(beta.probers) != 2 {
53			t.Fatalf("precondition: m.defs=%d beta.probers=%d, want 2 and 2", len(m.defs), len(beta.probers))
54		}
55	
56		// Simulate the wiring defect the guard defends against: beta's prober fan-out fell short
57		// of m.defs (exactly the shape a partial per-peer fan-out — reaching only SOME peers —
58		// would leave behind). Mutated directly (white-box) under m.mu, mirroring how the real
59		// fan-out sites hold the lock while mutating p.probers.
60		m.mu.Lock()
61		beta.probers = beta.probers[:1]
62		m.mu.Unlock()
63	
64		// RemovePath on the misaligned index ("deferred-c", defIdx=1) must fail fast with a
65		// descriptive wiring-defect error rather than panicking on beta.probers[2:] (out of range
66		// on a length-1 slice).
67		err := m.RemovePath("deferred-c")
68		if err == nil {
69			t.Fatal("RemovePath with a misaligned peer prober set succeeded, want a wiring-defect error")
70		}
71	
72		// Neither m.defs nor the (still-misaligned) beta.probers were mutated by the failed
73		// removal: the guard checks BEFORE mutating, so a caller could in principle repair the
74		// wiring and retry rather than being left with a half-spliced membership.
75		if len(m.defs) != 2 {
76			t.Fatalf("m.defs mutated by a failed removal: len=%d, want 2 (untouched)", len(m.defs))
77		}
78		if len(beta.probers) != 1 {
79			t.Fatalf("beta.probers mutated by a failed removal: len=%d, want 1 (untouched)", len(beta.probers))
80		}
81	}
82	
83	// TestRemoveDeferredPathInRangeMisalignedPeerProbersFailsFast is the ROUND-2 strengthening of
84	// removeDurableLocked's fail-fast alignment guard (D42): an INDEX-ONLY bound check
85	// (defIdx >= len(p.probers)) catches out-of-range divergence, but NOT an IN-RANGE one — a peer
86	// whose prober set has grown LONGER than m.defs (or fallen short at the tail while defIdx still
87	// happens to be in bounds) passes an index-only check silently and lets removeDurableLocked
88	// splice an entry that is no longer p.probers[defIdx]'s TRUE counterpart in m.defs, corrupting
89	// the durable per-peer alignment the whole fan-out (AddPath/promoteDeferredLocked) depends on —
90	// exactly the "silently splices the wrong entry" failure the guard's own doc comment claims to
91	// prevent. Before the length-based guard this RemovePath call would SUCCEED (silently
92	// mis-splicing, since defIdx=1 is well within bounds of a length-3 probers slice); after it,
93	// RemovePath returns a descriptive wiring-defect error instead, leaving m.defs and the
94	// (still-misaligned) beta.probers untouched.
95	func TestRemoveDeferredPathInRangeMisalignedPeerProbersFailsFast(t *testing.T) {
96		pskA := testKey(t, 0x95)
97		pskB := testKey(t, 0x96)
98		clk := newFakeClock()
99		paths := loopbackPaths(1) // boot path "a"
100		m, _, _ := newProbingMultipath(t, paths, pskA, clk)
101	
102		betaSched, betaProbers, betaFactory := concPeerWiring(t, paths, pskB, 0x0BAD3, clk)
103		if err := m.AddConcentratorPeer("beta", pskB, betaSched, betaProbers, betaFactory); err != nil {
104			t.Fatalf("AddConcentratorPeer: %v", err)
105		}
106		if _, _, err := m.Open(0); err != nil {
107			t.Fatalf("Open: %v", err)
108		}
109		t.Cleanup(func() { _ = m.Close() })
110	
111		// A runtime AddPath that DEFERS (EADDRNOTAVAIL): the already-shipped AddPath fan-out mints
112		// a Down prober for EVERY peer, keeping m.defs and every peer's probers aligned at length 2.
113		m.addPathListen = func(_ netip.Addr, _ uint16, _ string) (*net.UDPConn, error) {
114			return nil, syscall.EADDRNOTAVAIL
115		}
116		if err := m.AddPath(config.Path{Name: "deferred-c", SourceAddr: netip.MustParseAddr("127.0.0.1")}); err != nil {
117			t.Fatalf("AddPath (deferred): %v", err)
118		}
119	
120		beta := m.peersByName["beta"]
121		if len(m.defs) != 2 || len(beta.probers) != 2 {
122			t.Fatalf("precondition: m.defs=%d beta.probers=%d, want 2 and 2", len(m.defs), len(beta.probers))
123		}
124	
125		// Simulate an IN-RANGE wiring defect: beta's prober fan-out ran TWICE for one entry (or
126		// some other partial-fan-out artifact), leaving beta.probers LONGER than m.defs while
127		// defIdx=1 ("deferred-c") remains well within its bounds — the class of divergence an
128		// index-only bound check cannot see. Mutated directly (white-box) under m.mu, mirroring how
129		// the real fan-out sites hold the lock while mutating p.probers.
130		m.mu.Lock()
131		beta.probers = append(beta.probers, beta.probers[0])
132		m.mu.Unlock()
133	
134		// RemovePath on the in-range but length-divergent index ("deferred-c", defIdx=1) must fail
135		// fast with a wiring-defect error rather than silently splicing beta.probers[1] out from
136		// under the wrong path.
137		err := m.RemovePath("deferred-c")
138		if err == nil {
139			t.Fatal("RemovePath with an in-range (length-divergent) peer prober set succeeded, want a wiring-defect error")
140		}
141	
142		// Neither m.defs nor the (still-misaligned) beta.probers were mutated by the failed
143		// removal: the guard checks BEFORE mutating.
144		if len(m.defs) != 2 {
145			t.Fatalf("m.defs mutated by a failed removal: len=%d, want 2 (untouched)", len(m.defs))
146		}
147		if len(beta.probers) != 3 {
148			t.Fatalf("beta.probers mutated by a failed removal: len=%d, want 3 (untouched)", len(beta.probers))
149		}
150	}
151	
152	// TestReconcilePromotionFansViewAndSchedulerToEveryPeer is the T124/D42 core acceptance: on a
153	// 2-peer concentrator, a deferred boot path's PROMOTION (StartReconcileLoop's background
154	// reconcile, once the source_addr becomes assignable) must give EVERY bound peer — not just
155	// the primary — a receive-demux VIEW of the freshly-bound socket and a scheduler entry, and
156	// each peer's DATA must actually flow (demux + resequencer delivery) over the promoted path.
157	// Pre-fix, promoteDeferredLocked attached only the primary's view, leaving beta view-less: its
158	// frames on the promoted socket would never demux to it (this test's demuxInbound/DATA
159	// assertions fail pre-fix — beta's view is nil, so peerPathByName(beta, "deferred") is nil and
160	// the test fails before it can even attempt the DATA exchange).
161	func TestReconcilePromotionFansViewAndSchedulerToEveryPeer(t *testing.T) {
162		pskA := testKey(t, 0x81)
163		pskB := testKey(t, 0x82)
164		clk := newFakeClock()
165		paths := []config.Path{
166			{Name: "bindable", SourceAddr: netip.MustParseAddr("127.0.0.1")},
167			{Name: "deferred", SourceAddr: netip.MustParseAddr(unassignableSource)},
168		}
169		m, _, _ := newProbingMultipath(t, paths, pskA, clk)
170	
171		betaSched, betaProbers, betaFactory := concPeerWiring(t, paths, pskB, 0x0B00B00, clk)
172		if err := m.AddConcentratorPeer("beta", pskB, betaSched, betaProbers, betaFactory); err != nil {
173			t.Fatalf("AddConcentratorPeer: %v", err)
174		}
175		binder := &fakeDeferredBinder{}
176		m.deferredListen = binder.listen
177	
178		if _, _, err := m.Open(0); err != nil {
179			t.Fatalf("Open: %v", err)
180		}
181		t.Cleanup(func() { _ = m.Close() })
182	
183		primary := m.peerState
184		beta := m.peersByName["beta"]
185	
186		if len(m.deferred) != 1 {
187			t.Fatalf("after Open: deferred=%d, want 1", len(m.deferred))
188		}
189		if peerPathByName(primary, "deferred") != nil || peerPathByName(beta, "deferred") != nil {
190			t.Fatal("the deferred path already has a view before promotion")
191		}
192	
193		// The address becomes assignable; the background reconcile promotes it.
194		binder.arm()
195		m.reconcileDeferred()
196		if len(m.deferred) != 0 {
197			t.Fatalf("deferred after reconcile = %d, want 0 (promoted)", len(m.deferred))
198		}
199	
200		// Every bound peer got a VIEW of the promoted path.
201		primaryView := peerPathByName(primary, "deferred")
202		betaView := peerPathByName(beta, "deferred")
203		if primaryView == nil || betaView == nil {
204			t.Fatalf("promoted path missing a view: primary=%v beta=%v", pathNamesOfPeer(primary), pathNamesOfPeer(beta))
205		}
206		if primaryView.sharedPathState != betaView.sharedPathState {
207			t.Fatal("primary and beta's promoted views do not share the same socket")
208		}
209		if primaryView.prober == betaView.prober {
210			t.Fatal("primary and beta share ONE prober for the promoted path — the fan-out did not mint/reuse per-peer state")
211		}
212		assertProberKeyedOn(t, "primary promoted prober", primaryView.prober, pskA, pskB)
213		assertProberKeyedOn(t, "beta promoted prober", betaView.prober, pskB, pskA)
214	
215		// Both peers got a SCHEDULER entry too: exercised behaviourally below by proving each
216		// peer's DATA actually demuxes and delivers over the promoted (now-shared) socket — the
217		// "frames never demux to it" residual this task fixes. (A missing scheduler entry would
218		// leave the peer's view built but the path unschedulable for egress; the receive-side
219		// behavioural proof below is this test's DATA-flow acceptance.)
220		dataCodecA, err := frame.NewCodec(pskA)
221		if err != nil {
222			t.Fatalf("build peer A data codec: %v", err)
223		}
224		dataCodecB, err := frame.NewCodec(pskB)
225		if err != nil {
226			t.Fatalf("build peer B data codec: %v", err)
227		}
228	
229		srcA := netip.MustParseAddrPort("203.0.113.10:51820")
230		srcB := netip.MustParseAddrPort("198.51.100.10:51820")
231	
232		// Bind each source to its own peer through an authenticated PROBE over the PROMOTED
233		// socket (demuxInbound's production source-demux, entered via the primary's view — the
234		// same view the promoted path's single Bind-owned reader holds).
235		m.demuxInbound(primaryView, authProbe(t, pskA, primaryView.id, 1, clk), srcA)
236		if bound, ok := m.lookupPeerBySource(srcA); !ok || bound != primary {
237			t.Fatalf("srcA did not bind to the primary over the promoted path: bound=%v ok=%v", bound, ok)
238		}
239		m.demuxInbound(primaryView, authProbe(t, pskB, betaView.id, 1, clk), srcB)
240		if bound, ok := m.lookupPeerBySource(srcB); !ok || bound != beta {
241			t.Fatalf("srcB did not bind to beta over the promoted path: bound=%v ok=%v", bound, ok)
242		}
243	
244		// A DATA frame from each bound source demuxes to that peer's OWN resequencer over the
245		// promoted path — the acceptance's "both peers' DATA flows on the promoted path".
246		m.demuxInbound(primaryView, mustEncodeData(t, dataCodecA, 0, primaryView.id, "A-data"), srcA)
247		m.demuxInbound(primaryView, mustEncodeData(t, dataCodecB, 0, betaView.id, "B-data"), srcB)
248	
249		itA, ok := primary.resequencer.Load().Pop()
250		if !ok || string(itA.Payload) != "A-data" {
251			t.Fatalf("primary did not receive DATA over the promoted path (ok=%v)", ok)
252		}
253		itB, ok := beta.resequencer.Load().Pop()
254		if !ok || string(itB.Payload) != "B-data" {
255			t.Fatalf("beta did not receive DATA over the promoted path (ok=%v) — the promotion left beta's frames un-demuxed", ok)
256		}
257	}
258	
259	// TestConcentratorCloseOpenAfterDeferredPromoteRebuildsBothPeers is the T124 acceptance (c): a
260	// deferred path that is PROMOTED (via the background reconcile, exercising promoteDeferredLocked's
261	// fan-out) and THEN taken through a Close→Open cycle (an interface Down/Up, or the amneziawg
262	// engine's route-change cycle) must rebuild EVERY bound peer's view of it, from EVERY peer's
263	// still-aligned p.probers, without an out-of-range panic indexing p.probers[i] at Open (the D42
264	// scenario: a peer whose prober fan-out fell short of m.defs). This is a genuinely different
265	// path from TestConcentratorDeferredAddThenReopenFansPerPeerProbers (which reopens a path that
266	// was NEVER promoted): here the path IS promoted first, proving promoteDeferredLocked's own
267	// fan-out leaves durable state (m.defs / every peer's probers) exactly as aligned as it found it,
268	// so a subsequent reopen is unaffected by having gone through a promotion.
269	func TestConcentratorCloseOpenAfterDeferredPromoteRebuildsBothPeers(t *testing.T) {
270		pskA := testKey(t, 0x83)
271		pskB := testKey(t, 0x84)
272		clk := newFakeClock()
273		paths := loopbackPaths(1) // boot path "a"
274		m, _, _ := newProbingMultipath(t, paths, pskA, clk)
275	
276		betaSched, betaProbers, betaFactory := concPeerWiring(t, paths, pskB, 0x0C105E, clk)
277		if err := m.AddConcentratorPeer("beta", pskB, betaSched, betaProbers, betaFactory); err != nil {
278			t.Fatalf("AddConcentratorPeer: %v", err)
279		}
280		if _, _, err := m.Open(0); err != nil {
281			t.Fatalf("Open: %v", err)
282		}
283		t.Cleanup(func() { _ = m.Close() })
284	
285		// A runtime AddPath that DEFERS (EADDRNOTAVAIL), fanned to both peers by the already-shipped
286		// AddPath fix.
287		m.addPathListen = func(_ netip.Addr, _ uint16, _ string) (*net.UDPConn, error) {
288			return nil, syscall.EADDRNOTAVAIL
289		}
290		if err := m.AddPath(config.Path{Name: "runtime-deferred", SourceAddr: netip.MustParseAddr("127.0.0.1")}); err != nil {
291			t.Fatalf("AddPath (deferred): %v", err)
292		}
293		if len(m.defs) != 2 || len(m.deferred) != 1 {
294			t.Fatalf("after deferred AddPath: m.defs=%d m.deferred=%d, want 2 and 1", len(m.defs), len(m.deferred))
295		}
296	
297		// The address becomes assignable; the background reconcile PROMOTES it — exercising this
298		// task's fan-out fix — BEFORE the reopen below.
299		m.reconcileDeferred()
300		if len(m.deferred) != 0 {
301			t.Fatalf("deferred after reconcile = %d, want 0 (promoted)", len(m.deferred))
302		}
303		primary := m.peerState
304		beta := m.peersByName["beta"]
305		if peerPathByName(primary, "runtime-deferred") == nil || peerPathByName(beta, "runtime-deferred") == nil {
306			t.Fatalf("promotion did not fan a view to both peers before reopen: primary=%v beta=%v", pathNamesOfPeer(primary), pathNamesOfPeer(beta))
307		}
308	
309		// Close -> Open (an interface Down/Up, or a route-change cycle): must rebuild BOTH peers'
310		// views for EVERY durable path, including the just-promoted one, without panicking on
311		// p.probers[i] out of range.
312		if err := m.Close(); err != nil {
313			t.Fatalf("Close: %v", err)
314		}
315		if _, _, err := m.Open(0); err != nil {
316			t.Fatalf("reopen after a deferred-then-promoted path: %v", err)
317		}
318	
319		primary = m.peerState
320		beta = m.peersByName["beta"]
321		pa := peerPathByName(primary, "runtime-deferred")
322		pb := peerPathByName(beta, "runtime-deferred")
323		if pa == nil || pb == nil {
324			t.Fatalf("promoted-then-reopened path missing a view: primary=%v beta=%v", pathNamesOfPeer(primary), pathNamesOfPeer(beta))
325		}
326		if pa.prober == pb.prober {
327			t.Fatal("both peers share ONE prober after reopen — the durable membership desynced across the promote+reopen cycle")
328		}
329		assertProberKeyedOn(t, "primary reopened prober", pa.prober, pskA, pskB)
330		assertProberKeyedOn(t, "beta reopened prober", pb.prober, pskB, pskA)
331	}
332

667:		peers:             []*peerState{primary},
741:	m.peers = append(m.peers, p)

- **Did:** reviewed task T124 round 2 (diff 74f38b7..0ef749f on `implement/T124-r2`; merge-base verified == 74f38b7; round-1→round-2 delta isolated via `implement/T124-b`)
- **Achieved:** verdict approve; 0 criticisms / 0 questions
- **Discovered:** the round-1 criticism is resolved — the guard is now `len(p.probers) != len(m.defs)`, checked for every peer BEFORE any mutation (multipath.go:2773-2777), covering tail-short in-range, probers-longer-than-defs, and any length-changing non-tail desync. Empirical repro check: in a scratch worktree I reverted the guard to the exact round-1 index-only form (`defIdx >= len(p.probers)`); the new `TestRemoveDeferredPathInRangeMisalignedPeerProbersFailsFast` then failed with "RemovePath ... succeeded, want a wiring-defect error" (the right reason) while the round-1 tail-short test still passed — the new test isolates precisely the in-range class. Round-1→round-2 diff touches ONLY the guard + its doc comment + the 69-line new test; `reconcile.go` (the approved promotion fan-out core) is byte-identical between rounds. `m.peers[0]`-is-primary invariant confirmed at construction (multipath.go:667), so `probers[0].PathID()` and `readLoop(attached[0])` are sound. All checks green in the worktree: `go build`, `go vet`, `go test ./internal/bind/...`, `go test -race ./internal/bind/...`, `just lint` (0 issues).
